key_prs.author: jackchuma

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### What changed? Why?

Refactors the proposer proving pipeline by moving dispatch, collection, recovery, submit, and lifecycle responsibilities into focused proposer module types. `ProvingPipeline` now coordinates separate dispatcher and collector loops instead of owning the full proving state machine in one file, reducing `pipeline.rs` and keeping retry/restart handling with the component that owns it.

### Notes to reviewers

The functional surface stays inside `base-proposer`; public exports in `lib.rs` were intentionally adjusted for the split. `PipelineConfig` was folded into `DriverConfig`, `ProofCollectorRecoveryProvider` and `CollectedProof` were removed, and dispatcher runtime/tick result helper types are no longer exported. There are no remaining in-workspace users of those removed items.

The main review path is the interaction between `ProvingPipeline::run`, `ProofDispatcher`, `ProofCollectorOrchestrator`, `ProofRecovery`, and `PipelineHandle`.

### How has it been tested?

- `cargo test -p base-proposer`

key_prs.title: refactor(proposer): clean up pipeline

key_prs.author: refcell

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This adds a consensus metric that reports the seconds until the next scheduled upgrade activation. The metric uses runtime-aware upgrade activation timestamps, emits zero during a short activation grace window, and resets previously observed upgrade series after the next activation window passes. The included tests cover future countdowns, activation behavior, expired activations, and simultaneous upgrade timestamps.
commit_subjects: fix(consensus): wire upgrade countdown into unified nodes

key_prs.title: feat(consensus): Add Upgrade Countdown Metric

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This handles BOP-308 by extending bundle metering so forensic simulations can attribute gas to Beryl singleton precompiles and dynamic B-20 token address families. The metering inspector now uses the configured classifier instead of exact-address-only matching, while preserving standard precompile behavior. Parser and classifier tests cover Beryl names, singleton addresses, supported dynamic variants, and unsupported B-20 discriminants.

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Adds the execution-time **apply layer** (`apply.rs`) that mirrors `AccountConfiguration`'s write semantics for the three `AccountChange` variants, plus the storage-write plumbing it needs. This is the `apply` half that the EVM integration (`core.rs`) will call after authorization to actually mutate account-configuration state.

- **CreateEntry** — CREATE2 address derivation + a deferred deployment effect (`CreatedAccount`) for the EVM layer to deploy
- **ConfigChange** — sequence advancement, actor authorize/revoke, and policy-slot writes
- **Delegation** — a deferred delegation-indicator code-write effect (`DelegationEffect`)

Storage state transitions (actor config, account state, policy slots) are written via `AccountConfigurationStorage`; account-**code** modifications (CREATE2 deploy, delegation indicator) are returned as effects so storage and code concerns stay separated and the EVM layer owns code writes.

Supporting changes:
- `ActorConfig`/`AccountState` gain `to_word` packing (the inverse of the existing `from_word`), cross-checked against Solidity packing in tests.
- `AccountConfigurationStorage` gains `set_actor_config`/`clear_actor_config`, `set_account...

key_prs.author: jackchuma

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### What changed? Why?

Refactors the proposer proof collector into explicit collector state, target retry state, and a `ProofCollectorOrchestrator` that owns prover-service polling, dispatch retries, and sequential submit handling.

Keeps the proving pipeline responsible for running dispatcher and collector loops while moving collector-specific retry/session behavior out of the pipeline.

### Notes to reviewers

The main behavior to review is retry/session preservation around failed proofs, missing prover-service sessions, discard retries, and submit-timeout restarts.

### How has it been tested?

Not run.

key_prs.title: refactor(proposer): clean up proof collector

key_prs.author: refcell

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This removes the consensus RPC metrics feature from the batcher service metrics feature set. The batcher still uses consensus RPC clients, but no longer initializes consensus gossip gauges that can make non-P2P batcher pods emit zero peer-count metrics. This prevents the shadow batcher from matching low P2P peer-count monitors as a false signal.
commit_subjects: fix(batcher): avoid consensus metrics in batcher

key_prs.author: jackchuma

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### What changed? Why?

Refactors the proof proposer TEE dispatch path so `ProofDispatcher` owns prover-service submission directly and `ProposerProofAdapter` only handles proof request/result conversion.

Removes the standalone proof requester dispatcher wrapper, returns accepted session IDs directly, and keeps AWS Nitro session ID derivation in the adapter.

### Notes to reviewers

The behavior this preserves is covered in the existing dispatcher/collector tests: root-derived sessions remain idempotent, discard retries still use retry-specific session IDs, and existing `l1_head` conflicts for the same session are accepted.

### How has it been tested?

- `git diff --check refactor/clean-pipeline...HEAD`
- `cargo test -p base-proposer`

key_prs.title: refactor(proposer): clean up proof adapter

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Add a run-local last-known-active signer cache for registrar discovery cycles.
- Preserve cached signers and mark their instance unresolved when an instance disappears from otherwise successful discovery output.
- Evict cached missing instances after `INSTANCE_CACHE_TTL_CYCLES` so real removals eventually cancel proofs and deregister signers.
- Add driver regression tests for missing-instance protection, TTL eviction, TTL reset on refresh, and cold-start empty discovery behavior.

## Trade-off
- The 5-cycle TTL reduces transient discovery flakes causing proof cancellation or orphan deregistration.
- If the TTL is too short, partial discovery glitches can still leak through.
- If the TTL is too long, genuine instance removal cleanup is delayed.

## Testing
- `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo clippy -p base-proof-tee-registrar --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar`
- Local caveat: exact cargo commands without `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1` fail on this machine because `risc0-sys` invokes `xcrun metal`, and the `metal` developer tool is not installed or not on PATH.
commit_subjects: fix(registrar): clarify cache ttl on resolve errors
fix(registrar): keep cached signers active on resolve error
fix(registrar): update cache tests for endpoint mock

merged.title: fix(registrar): protect cached discovery signers

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Record terminal prover-service success and duration metrics when worker `submit_proof` newly completes a job.
- Split idempotent `submit_proof` replays into `AlreadyCompleted` so repeated worker submissions return the stored result without double-counting terminal metrics.
- Reuse the same terminal metric helper for expired-claim reaper failures and stuck-request permanent failures so `proof_requests_completed` and `proof_request_duration_ms` stay aligned on normal terminal paths.
- Route fraudulent ZK challenge checkpoint validation through the shared output validator path so Path 2 emits the existing validation latency, error, and invalid-root metrics.
- Document the metric behavior, why replay does not count again, and the relevant test commands in the prover-service and challenger READMEs.

## Why
The existing metric definitions were present, but some terminal paths did not emit them. Successful worker completions skipped `zk_prover_service.proof_requests_completed` and `zk_prover_service.proof_request_duration_ms`, and the fraudulent-ZK-challenge path computed one checkpoint directly instead of using the shared instrumented validator. That made dashboards under...

## Summary
- Changes `enclave_signerAttestation` to accept an optional per-signer nonce vector.
- Sends one attestation RPC per prover instance with all deterministic signer nonces instead of one RPC per signer.
- Validates nonce vector length in the Nitro host RPC handler and routes each nonce to the matching enclave transport.

## Tangible value
This removes the N RPC calls / N^2 attestation response work introduced by deterministic per-signer nonces in #3674, while keeping one nonce per signer and preserving stateless Boundless recovery.

## Stacking
- Stacked on #3674 (`registrar-attestation-nonce-check`). Rebase onto `main` after #3674 lands.

## Tests
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-nitro-host signer_attestation`
- `just check::clippy-affected-ci "origin/registrar-attestation-nonce-check"`

## Summary
- Select the proof request L1 head from rollup sync status instead of always using finalized L1.
- Use finalized L1 for finalized targets and safe L1 for safe targets when non-finalized proposals are enabled.
- Add request-build logging for selected L1/L2 coverage and bump proposer proof-session namespace to avoid stale v1 sessions.

## Tests
- cargo test -p base-proposer

## Why this PR exists

Grype scans on our Go bake images showed most remaining CVEs come from the **Go standard library** baked into release binaries — not application dependencies we can patch separately. The fix is to bump the Go builder image regularly.

## What we tried first

The smallest change was to bump `golang:1.26.4` in the Dockerfiles only. That would ship fixed stdlib in production images.

The problem: CircleCI still runs `go test`, lint, and fuzz on the old toolchain from `mise.toml` and `go.mod`. We would be testing one Go version and shipping another. Regressions that only show up under 1.26 would not be caught in CI. So we aligned the toolchain pins too.

## How the PR grew (in order)

### 1. Docker builder bump

Updated Go builder pins in:
- `ops/docker/op-stack-go/Dockerfile` (all 15 bake targets)
- `ops/docker/deployment-utils/Dockerfile`
- `cannon/Dockerfile.diff`
- `rust/kona/docker/fpvm-prestates/cannon-repro.dockerfile`

This is the core change: rebuilt images pick up 1.26.4 stdlib CVE fixes.

### 2. Toolchain alignment

Bumped `mise.toml` (`go = "1.26.4"`) and `go.mod` (`go 1.26.0`, `toolchain go1.26.4`). Ran `go mod tidy`; `go.sum` unchanged.

## Summary

This is a narrow follow-up to PR #20823. It keeps the cold-start retry behavior while the supernode is waiting for VNs/SafeDB to become ready, but treats failures from the actual configured log-backfill range as fatal.

## Rationale

Once every chain has produced a SafeDB first entry, the supernode has selected the startup handoff and has begun fetching historical logs. If that historical range cannot be served, retrying the same range forever does not make progress and contradicts the intended hard-fail behavior for unavailable backfill history.

## Changes

- Wrap `runColdStartBackfill` failures in a sentinel `errColdStartBackfill`.
- Let `waitForColdStartInit` return that sentinel instead of retrying indefinitely.
- Add a unit test proving `Start` exits when the backfill range cannot be fetched after SafeDB readiness.

## Validation

- `go test ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/interop`
- `git diff --check`

Part 1 of 2. Splits the original combined removal (#21255) into a client-first, server-second sequence so the rollout is safe under a rolling upgrade.

Relates to: https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/issues/21154

## Why split client and server

If we removed both at once, a node that upgrades early would stop *serving* req-resp payloads while peers still running the old client are mid-catch-up. By removing the **client** first and keeping the **server**, upgraded nodes continue to serve payloads-by-number to any peer that still requests them. Once everyone is past this change (nobody requests via req-resp anymore), the follow-up PR removes the server.

- **This PR (client):** → `develop`
- **Follow-up (server):** #21499 (stacked on this branch); merge after this.

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Requires `brotli-decompressor` `>= 5.0.1` (up from the previously-resolved `5.0.0`), which pulls in [dropbox/rust-brotli-decompressor#44](https://github.com/dropbox/rust-brotli-decompressor/pull/44).

That PR fixes a missing `break` in the decoder's `BROTLI_STATE_METABLOCK_DONE` arm: the RFC 7932 §9.2 padding check (the unused bits in the final byte must be zero) set `BROTLI_DECODER_ERROR_FORMAT_PADDING_2` but fell through into `BROTLI_STATE_DONE`, where `WriteRingBuffer` unconditionally overwrote `result` with `SUCCESS`. So corrupt brotli streams decoded as `Ok` with output.

Go (`andybalholm/brotli`, used by op-node) and the C reference (`libbrotlidec`) both reject these streams as `PADDING_2`, so **5.0.0 was a Go-vs-Rust derivation divergence vector**: op-node rejects the channel while kona would have accepted it — the same class of consensus risk as #19333.

## How the version is pinned

Rather than a lockfile-only bump (which a future lockfile regeneration could undo), `brotli-decompressor` is now a **direct** workspace dependency floored at `^5.0.1`, so it can never resolve below the fix. kona's decoder already only used the decode API (`BrotliDecompressStream`...

`op-core/superchain` `//go:embed`s a gitignored `superchain-configs.zip` (built only by the `prep-superchain` CI job / `just build-superchain-go` locally), so any package that *transitively* links it fails to compile — `pattern superchain-configs.zip: no matching files found` — until the bundle is built.

Documenting it from both angles so the next person finds it:

- **`docs/ai/ci-ops.md`** — the CI-failure symptom + how to provision the bundle per job (attach the `prep-superchain` workspace, an in-job `just build-superchain-go`, or have the recipe build it).
- **`docs/ai/go-dev.md`** — the local-dev angle: the `just` build/test/lint targets prep it automatically (they depend on `build-superchain-go`); for a bare `go build`/`go test` run `just build-superchain-go` (or `just sync-superchain` to prep all Go + Rust bundles).

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Requires `brotli-decompressor` `>= 5.0.1` (up from the previously-resolved `5.0.0`), which pulls in [dropbox/rust-brotli-decompressor#44](https://github.com/dropbox/rust-brotli-decompressor/pull/44).

That PR fixes a missing `break` in the decoder's `BROTLI_STATE_METABLOCK_DONE` arm: the RFC 7932 §9.2 padding check (the unused bits in the final byte must be zero) set `BROTLI_DECODER_ERROR_FORMAT_PADDING_2` but fell through into `BROTLI_STATE_DONE`, where `WriteRingBuffer` unconditionally overwrote `result` with `SUCCESS`. So corrupt brotli streams decoded as `Ok` with output.

Go (`andybalholm/brotli`, used by op-node) and the C reference (`libbrotlidec`) both reject these streams as `PADDING_2`, so **5.0.0 was a Go-vs-Rust derivation divergence vector**: op-node rejects the channel while kona would have accepted it — the same class of consensus risk as #19333.

## How the version is pinned

Rather than a lockfile-only bump (which a future lockfile regeneration could undo), `brotli-decompressor` is now a **direct** workspace dependency floored at `^5.0.1`, so it can never resolve below the fix. kona's decoder already only used the decode API (`BrotliDecompressStream`...

Relates to: https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/issues/21154

## Why split client and server

If we removed both at once, a node that upgrades early would stop *serving* req-resp payloads while peers still running the old client are mid-catch-up. Once everyone is past this change (nobody requests via req-resp anymore), the follow-up PR removes the server.

- **Follow-up (server):** #21499 (stacked on this branch); merge after this.

Follow-up to #21275. Relaxes `L2BlockInfo::from_block_and_genesis` off OpTxEnvelope so a downstream chain (Celo, CIP-64 envelope) can reuse kona-driver unmodified.

- **`kona-protocol`**: relax `L2BlockInfo::from_block_and_genesis` from `T: Typed2718 + AsRef<OpTxEnvelope>` to `T: Typed2718 + OpTransaction`, reading the first tx via `tx.as_deposit()`. That's all the function needs, and `AsRef<OpTxEnvelope>` is unimplementable for extended envelopes (no inner `OpTxEnvelope` to borrow).
- **`op-alloy`** (in-monorepo): add delegating blanket `OpTransaction` impls for `Recovered<T>` and the rpc `Transaction<T>` wrapper, mirroring the existing `Extended<B, T>` impl. No in-tree caller passes these (all lower to `OpTxEnvelope` first); they exist so a downstream envelope gets `OpTransaction for Recovered<_>` / `Transaction<_>` for free once it implements `OpTransaction`.

These impls can't live downstream — the orphan rule forbids `impl OpTransaction for Recovered<CeloEnvelope>` from a downstream crate (both trait and wrapper are foreign, the local envelope is covered).

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This is a narrow follow-up to PR #20823. It keeps the cold-start retry behavior while the supernode is waiting for VNs/SafeDB to become ready, but treats failures from the actual configured log-backfill range as fatal.

## Rationale

Once every chain has produced a SafeDB first entry, the supernode has selected the startup handoff and has begun fetching historical logs. If that historical range cannot be served, retrying the same range forever does not make progress and contradicts the intended hard-fail behavior for unavailable backfill history.

## Changes

- Wrap `runColdStartBackfill` failures in a sentinel `errColdStartBackfill`.
- Let `waitForColdStartInit` return that sentinel instead of retrying indefinitely.
- Add a unit test proving `Start` exits when the backfill range cannot be fetched after SafeDB readiness.

## Validation

- `go test ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/interop`
- `git diff --check`

`op-core/superchain` `//go:embed`s a gitignored `superchain-configs.zip` (built only by the `prep-superchain` CI job / `just build-superchain-go` locally), so any package that *transitively* links it fails to compile — `pattern superchain-configs.zip: no matching files found` — until the bundle is built.

This bit #21487 (the op-geth superchain decoupling) hard: a **Go-only** change red-washed a swath of unrelated-looking CI jobs (contracts-bedrock, fuzz-golang, rust-e2e) all at compile time, while passing locally and in review because the developer already has the zip on disk. Documenting it from both angles so the next person finds it:

- **`docs/ai/ci-ops.md`** — the CI-failure symptom + how to provision the bundle per job (attach the `prep-superchain` workspace, an in-job `just build-superchain-go`, or have the recipe build it).
- **`docs/ai/go-dev.md`** — the local-dev angle: the `just` build/test/lint targets prep it automatically (they depend on `build-superchain-go`); for a bare `go build`/`go test` run `just build-superchain-go` (or `just sync-superchain` to prep all Go + Rust bundles).

Docs-only.

**Phase 0** of the SDM → optimism-premium extraction: introduce a refund-inspector seam in the monorepo so *which* `PostExecRefundInspector` runs becomes a construction-time choice, while keeping behavior byte-for-byte identical. **Stacked on #21502** (pre-work); review the diff against that branch.

## Summary

Two consensus-surface pre-work fixes for the SDM PostExec (`0x7D`) machinery, landed ahead of the SDM → optimism-premium extraction (the §4 *Pre-work (remaining)* items: snapshot `PostExecMode` once, and enforce `saw_post_exec_tx` in `finish()`). Both are small, independently reviewable, and behavior-preserving for valid blocks.

### 1. `fix(alloy-op-evm)`: reject Verify blocks missing the trailing `0x7D`
A `Verify`-mode block whose normal txs apply the claimed SDM refunds but which **never carries the trailing `0x7D` post-exec tx** previously passed `finish()`. Per-tx settlement drains the verifier `remaining` map as the refunded txs commit (`block/mod.rs` `commit_transaction`), so an absent `0x7D` was invisible to the existing unconsumed-entries check. `finish()` now also rejects `Verifying { saw_post_exec_tx: false }` via the new `PostExecState::missing_post_exec_tx()`.

### 2. `fix(op-reth)`: snapshot `PostExecMode` once per build (opt-in TOCTOU)
`build()` read the runtime-mutable SDM production opt-in twice — once inside `block_builder()` (mode selection) and again when deciding to append the `0x7D`. An admin-RPC toggle between the reads could yield a block with...

Stacked on #21497 (base branch `einar/fix-reth-optimism-evm-tests`). Merge #21497 first.

## Problem

A crate's tests can compile in the `rust-tests` job (`cargo nextest run --workspace --all-features`) purely because Cargo feature unification enables a feature that a *sibling* crate turns on. Built in isolation, those tests fail. The existing `rust-cargo-hack` gate runs `--no-dev-deps` and never compiles test code, so it misses this entire class — which is how #21497 (reth-optimism-evm relying on `superchain-configs`) slipped through.

## What this does

1. **Fixes the 7 crates** currently affected (found via `cargo hack check --workspace --tests --all-features`):
   - `reth-optimism-{consensus,payload-builder,txpool,rpc,flashblocks}` — tests use `OP_MAINNET` / `OP_SEPOLIA` / `OpChainSpecBuilder::optimism_mainnet()`, behind chainspec's non-default `superchain-configs` (dev-dep override for consensus, where chainspec is otherwise a production-only dep).
   - `kona-proof` — needs `alloy-eips/kzg` (`env_settings`) and `rand/thread_rng` (`rand::rng()`).
   - `kona-providers-local` — `#[tokio::test]` needs tokio `macros` + `rt`.
2. **Adds a regression gate**: a `hack-tests-all` just...

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Requires `brotli-decompressor` `>= 5.0.1` (up from the previously-resolved `5.0.0`), which pulls in [dropbox/rust-brotli-decompressor#44](https://github.com/dropbox/rust-brotli-decompressor/pull/44).

That PR fixes a missing `break` in the decoder's `BROTLI_STATE_METABLOCK_DONE` arm: the RFC 7932 §9.2 padding check (the unused bits in the final byte must be zero) set `BROTLI_DECODER_ERROR_FORMAT_PADDING_2` but fell through into `BROTLI_STATE_DONE`, where `WriteRingBuffer` unconditionally overwrote `result` with `SUCCESS`. So corrupt brotli streams decoded as `Ok` with output.

Go (`andybalholm/brotli`, used by op-node) and the C reference (`libbrotlidec`) both reject these streams as `PADDING_2`, so **5.0.0 was a Go-vs-Rust derivation divergence vector**: op-node rejects the channel while kona would have accepted it — the same class of consensus risk as #19333.

## How the version is pinned

Rather than a lockfile-only bump (which a future lockfile regeneration could undo), `brotli-decompressor` is now a **direct** workspace dependency floored at `^5.0.1`, so it can never resolve below the fix. kona's decoder already only used the decode API (`BrotliDecompressStream`...

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Part 1 of 2. Splits the original combined removal (#21255) into a client-first, server-second sequence so the rollout is safe under a rolling upgrade.

Relates to: https://github.com/ethereum-optimism/optimism/issues/21154

## Why split client and server

If we removed both at once, a node that upgrades early would stop *serving* req-resp payloads while peers still running the old client are mid-catch-up. By removing the **client** first and keeping the **server**, upgraded nodes continue to serve payloads-by-number to any peer that still requests them. Once everyone is past this change (nobody requests via req-resp anymore), the follow-up PR removes the server.

- **This PR (client):** → `develop`
- **Follow-up (server):** #21499 (stacked on this branch); merge after this.

Removes the client side of the Req/Resp CL P2P sync protocol; keeps the serving side intact.

- Delete `SyncClient` (and client-only helpers) from `op-node/p2p/sync.go`; the file now holds only `ReqRespServer` and the shared stream/protocol helpers it needs. A server-only `TestMutexUnlocks` is retained in `sync_test.go`.
- Strip client activation/wiring from `NodeP2P`. The serving side is still r...

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Follow-up to #21275. Relaxes `L2BlockInfo::from_block_and_genesis` off OpTxEnvelope so a downstream chain (Celo, CIP-64 envelope) can reuse kona-driver unmodified.

- **`kona-protocol`**: relax `L2BlockInfo::from_block_and_genesis` from `T: Typed2718 + AsRef<OpTxEnvelope>` to `T: Typed2718 + OpTransaction`, reading the first tx via `tx.as_deposit()`. That's all the function needs, and `AsRef<OpTxEnvelope>` is unimplementable for extended envelopes (no inner `OpTxEnvelope` to borrow).
- **`op-alloy`** (in-monorepo): add delegating blanket `OpTransaction` impls for `Recovered<T>` and the rpc `Transaction<T>` wrapper, mirroring the existing `Extended<B, T>` impl. No in-tree caller passes these (all lower to `OpTxEnvelope` first); they exist so a downstream envelope gets `OpTransaction for Recovered<_>` / `Transaction<_>` for free once it implements `OpTransaction`.

These impls can't live downstream — the orphan rule forbids `impl OpTransaction for Recovered<CeloEnvelope>` from a downstream crate (both trait and wrapper are foreign, the local envelope is covered).

## API impact

Semver-visible: a `T` implementing `AsRef<OpTxEnvelope>` but not `OpTransaction` no longer com...

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Problem

`cargo check -p reth-optimism-evm --tests` fails to compile:

```
error[E0432]: unresolved import `reth_optimism_chainspec::OP_MAINNET`
error[E0599]: no function or associated item named `optimism_mainnet` found for struct `OpChainSpecBuilder`
```

The crate's test modules reference `OP_MAINNET` and `OpChainSpecBuilder::optimism_mainnet()`, both of which were moved behind the `superchain-configs` feature of `reth-optimism-chainspec` during the superchain decoupling work (#21397 / #21487). That feature is not enabled by default, so the lib compiles but `--tests` does not.

## Fix

Add a dev-dependency on `reth-optimism-chainspec` with `superchain-configs` enabled, so the tests compile against the real OP Mainnet chain spec. Test semantics are unchanged (still exercising regolith/canyon activation against OP Mainnet); only the test build's feature surface changes.

## Verification

- `cargo check -p reth-optimism-evm --tests` → passes
- `cargo test -p reth-optimism-evm` → 29 passed; 0 failed

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This is a narrow follow-up to PR #20823. It keeps the cold-start retry behavior while the supernode is waiting for VNs/SafeDB to become ready, but treats failures from the actual configured log-backfill range as fatal.

## Rationale

Once every chain has produced a SafeDB first entry, the supernode has selected the startup handoff and has begun fetching historical logs. If that historical range cannot be served, retrying the same range forever does not make progress and contradicts the intended hard-fail behavior for unavailable backfill history.

## Changes

- Wrap `runColdStartBackfill` failures in a sentinel `errColdStartBackfill`.
- Let `waitForColdStartInit` return that sentinel instead of retrying indefinitely.
- Add a unit test proving `Start` exits when the backfill range cannot be fetched after SafeDB readiness.

## Validation

- `go test ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/interop`
- `git diff --check`
